description_long: This is a two-story restaurant building with a weathered, gray stucco exterior accented by exposed red brickwork and ornate architectural details. The front facade features a row of arched doorways on the ground floor, each with a red awning, and a balcony with decorative wrought iron railing above. Wooden shutters cover the upper windows, and the roofline is adorned with classical finials and a central circular window. Graffiti and street art are visible on the side and rear walls, adding an urban, lived-in character. A hanging sign projects from the front, and electrical wiring is visible along the facade, suggesting an older, possibly historic structure in a city setting.
description: A weathered, two-story restaurant with arched doorways, red awnings, wooden shutters, and ornate brick and stucco detailing, featuring graffiti and a hanging sign.
description_view_0: Side view showing gray stucco, exposed brick patches, graffiti, and a hanging sign near the front corner.
description_view_1: Front view with arched doorways, red awnings, balcony with iron railing, wooden shutters, and ornate roofline.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with more graffiti, exposed brick, and a mostly blank wall with some architectural detailing.
description_view_3: Rear view showing a large graffiti mural, exposed brickwork, and minimal architectural ornamentation.
attribute_signage: A rectangular hanging sign with a greenish patina is mounted near the front corner of the building; graffiti tags and murals are visible on the side and rear walls.
attribute_facade_design: The facade features a combination of weathered gray stucco and exposed red brick, with ornate cornices and finials along the roofline. The ground floor has arched doorways with red awnings, while the upper floor displays wooden shutters and a decorative balcony railing. Graffiti and urban wear add character to the exterior, blending classical and urban elements.
